hypothesis: A Transformer encoder–decoder model employing 150 cm⁻¹ patch-based spectrum embeddings, a jointly trained spectral reconstruction alignment loss, and multitask training across concentration ratios, when pretrained on ~790,000 simulated MD spectra and fine-tuned on 3,453 NIST spectra, can accurately and robustly identify molecular constituents from binary and ternary IR mixture spectra (including imbalanced ratios and experimental ATR‑IR mixtures), as evidenced by high Top‑k accuracy, low MAE, and high Tanimoto similarity to ground-truth components.
